_meta.description: Authoritative UWC truck-booking reference data extracted from the spec workbook. Single source of truth for database seeding, code, and validation. The .xlsx remains the original source document — keep both in sync.

operating_window.note: Operating hours (AUTO DISPATCH LOGIC). The end moved 18:00 -> 02:00 on 2026-07-17 (Mr. Teh item 12: 'can pickup time allow set until 2AM instead of 6pm'), so an operating day runs 07:00 to 02:00 the NEXT calendar day; a 00:00-02:00 pickup belongs to the previous calendar day's operating day. The window bounds both the pickup time and the estimated completion. OPEN QUESTION: whether 02:00 is the latest a truck may LEAVE or the time it must be BACK - the code assumes the latter.
operating_window.rate_tier_note: Distinct from the window: the PEAK rate band is the workbook's 'Weekday 8am - 6pm' (INTERNAL LORRY RATE). Off-peak rates apply on public holidays, Saturday/Sunday, and weekdays at/after 18:00 OR before 08:00 - the two rate tables partition the day, so the early-morning hours the widened window opens up are off-peak.

_meta.incentive_formula: incentive_RM = (accumulated_daily_trip_incentive_points - daily_deduction) x rate_per_point. Worked examples in the workbook: PLX 2406 assigned to Kulim earns 11 x 3 = RM 33; a driver accumulating 20 points in a day nets 20 - 2 = 18 points.
notes: Truck rate_per_point is the 'Entitled Claim per point' column; weekday vs off-peak differ for PPE 1804 (10 vs 12), PLX 2406 and PND 1888 (11 vs 13). Others are equal across both.
destination_points are the first-trip-of-day point values per destination (the yellow 'trip incentive point' row).
trucks[].priority_zones is the coverage of the truck's assigned driver (see driver_assignments, the primary source). '4 Wheel' has no driver in the workbook, so it has no priority zones.
cargo.pallet_sizes[].factor is the agreed 4x4-equivalent capacity convention (mirrors api/src/lib/pallets.ts); the workbook lists the pallet sizes but not the factors.

Kuala Lumpur is zone code KL (8 points, the furthest) — a real spec destination, wired like the other zones. Johor & Selangor appear only in the REQUESTOR destination dropdown with no points or zone code in the rate sheet, so they are kept OUT of the authoritative datasets above and live in the separate 'extensions' block at the end of this file (added as 8-point JH/SL zones).

Rate reconciliation (2026-06-30): trucks[].weekday_rate / offpeak_rate / max_pallets were verified against Mr. Teh's authoritative INTERNAL LORRY RATE sheet — all 7 plates and both rate tiers match exactly.

daily_deduction: CONFIRMED for all 7 plates by the authoritative INTERNAL LORRY RATE sheet ('Daily Deduction POINT' column in References/'TRUCK BOOKING SYSTEM (YS).xlsx') — PLX 2, PND 2, PRJ 3, PQL 3, PPE 3, PRH 2, 4 Wheel 2. These values match the sheet exactly; not a guess.

4 Wheel (Generic): weekday AND off-peak rates are both 11 — CONFIRMED by the INTERNAL LORRY RATE sheet, which lists a 4 Wheel row (= 11) in BOTH the peak and off-peak tables (not a default). Still OPEN (a genuine gap the workbook itself leaves): '4 Wheel' has no assigned driver and no priority_zones in the workbook, so whether it is a dispatchable 7th truck or an external/standby fallback is undecided.

driver_assignments names/employee numbers are NEUTRAL PLACEHOLDERS: the real identities are NDA data and live in the gitignored References/uwc-spec.private.json overlay (matched by truck plate), which seed.ts applies when present. Trucks, zones and rates here are unchanged spec data.

cargo.pallet_factor_rule: factor = width x height / 16 (a 4x4 pallet = 16 square units = 1 slot). The five original factors all satisfy this exactly, which is why the five added on 2026-07-17 (item 2) are derived from it rather than guessed. Totals round to 4 dp: every factor is a sixteenth, so the finest is 1/16 = 0.0625.

pallet_sizes.note: Added item 2. UNCONFIRMED as a pallet type — pending Mr. Teh.

Added item 2. UNCONFIRMED as a pallet type: 0.0625 implies ~256 to a PLX 2406, which is not a pallet-shaped number — may be boxes/crates belonging under 'carton' with an estimated_pallets count. Pending Mr. Teh.

non_pallet.note: Counted as a quantity of cartons; does not occupy a 4x4 pallet slot.
Free-form 'Others' size described in the remark; admin judges capacity.

driver_assignments.name: Driver 2
driver_assignments.notes: Assign A1 & A2 if PLX 2406 is not available.

driver_assignments.name: Driver 6
driver_assignments.notes: If A1 & A2 with less than 2 pallets of 4x4, give the priority.

extensions._note: ⚠ PLACEHOLDER / UNCONFIRMED — NOT authoritative spec data. Johor & Selangor appear in the REQUESTOR INTERFACE destination dropdown but carry NO zone code or incentive points in the INTERNAL LORRY RATE sheet. The 8-point values below are a TEST PLACEHOLDER (matching Kuala Lumpur, the long-haul reference) so JH/SL bookings score during testing instead of throwing ZONE_POINTS_MISSING. They MUST be replaced with Mr. Teh's real values before go-live and must not be mistaken for confirmed spec values. Seeded by seed.ts into the same Zone/DestinationRate tables as the spec zones (marked placeholder there too); kept in this separate 'extensions' block so the spec-vs-placeholder boundary stays clear.
